source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/TimeSeries/MAAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D6 STRUCTURAL oracle (D5 seeded-scalar assertions removed; see below). MAAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over an MA(1) MovingAverage model, then builds the posterior forecast ensemble (mode/mean/credible band). training_time_steps pinned to 15; curve_length = 20 + 3 = 23 (the deterministic invariant, reproduced exactly across C++/R/Python AND the real-RMC.BestFit emitter, tools/oracle_emitter mirroring ma_analysis::run()). D6 REALITY: the emitter does NOT reproduce the D5 self-computed seeded scalars (forecast mode/mean/credible band, AIC/BIC/DIC/RMSE) -- the ported C++ MA analysis and the real C# MAAnalysis micro-diverge on this short chaotic DEMCzs chain (AIC/BIC agree to ~0.02%, but the forecast band and point estimate drift). This matches the D6 brief's design statement that the time-series analyses are STRUCTURAL oracles only -- no numeric MCMC oracle exists upstream. Unlike AR, the MA forecast step-0 value is itself a seeded posterior forecast (not a deterministic first observation), so only curve_length survives as a cross-reproducible invariant. thinning_interval pinned to 1; short run (iterations 100, output_length 400).
